summary: Matched the Mr. Game & Watch breath item object by promoting itgamewatchbreath.c from NonMatching to Matching, adding its state table and proper prototypes, and moving the it_802C720C declaration out of the generic it_27CF header into the item-specific itgamewatchbreath.h. The PR also made small matching-oriented rewrites in item utility code, including loop structure and expression splitting, and fixed cross-header includes so ftGw_AttackAir.c uses the breath item header directly.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itgamewatchbreath.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Added the breath item state table definition it_803F7938 in itgamewatchbreath.c with two entries pointing to it_802C744C.
Added item-specific declarations to itgamewatchbreath.h for it_802C720C, it_802C7340, it_802C738C, it_802C73AC, it_802C7424, and it_802C744C, and kept the extern declaration for it_803F7938.
Removed the it_802C720C prototype from the broader it_27CF.h and it_27CF.dox locations, consolidating it under itgamewatchbreath.h.
Updated ftGw_AttackAir.c to include it/items/itgamewatchbreath.h directly.
Adjusted it_802C720C to use the signature HSD_GObj* it_802C720C(HSD_GObj* parent, Vec3* pos, Fighter_Part part, float dir), matching the definition and review feedback about the parent gobj type.
Changed it_802C744C from taking void* to Item_GObj*, improving type specificity for the breath item callback.
Inserted padding locals in it_802C7340 and it_802C744C, likely to influence stack layout for matching.
Rewrote it_802758D4 in it_2725.c from an unrolled hitbox-state check to a four-iteration loop with a break, and changed the damage comparison form to if (damage <= hitcapsule->damage).
Split the random integer expression in it_8026F8B4 from a single addition assignment into two statements, assigning HSD_Randi(it_804D6D28->x13C) first and adding it_804D6D28->x140 afterward.
smart_moves: Moved breath-specific APIs out of the generic it_27CF header into itgamewatchbreath.h, reducing header ambiguity and making call sites include the item-specific dependency.
Used Fighter_Part in the it_802C720C prototype instead of a raw s32, which preserves semantic information at the function boundary.
Kept the first it_802C720C argument generic as HSD_GObj* because review evidence indicated the parent object may be a Game & Watch fighter gobj rather than an item gobj.
Restored a compact loop form in it_802758D4 while preserving the early-exit behavior for detecting any enabled hit capsule.
Applied matching-oriented source transformations such as expression splitting and explicit padding locals rather than changing higher-level behavior.
decomp_lessons: When a function definition and header disagree on gobj type, prefer the type supported by the definition and call context; forcing Item_GObj* can break docs or type consistency if the argument is actually a parent fighter object.
Item-specific symbols such as it_802C720C for Game & Watch breath should live in the item-specific header when possible, rather than remaining in broad aggregate headers like it_27CF.h.
Replacing raw integer parameters with domain types such as Fighter_Part improves decomp readability and can clarify caller intent without changing ABI.
Loops can match where unrolled source was previously used; a break after finding the first enabled hit capsule can reproduce control flow while keeping the code concise.
Simple arithmetic expression grouping can matter for matching: splitting a call result assignment from a later addition may affect generated code.
Padding locals are sometimes used deliberately in matching PRs to align stack layout; their presence should be treated as a matching tactic unless later struct/type cleanup removes the need.
naming_conventions: Game & Watch item headers use item-specific names such as itgamewatchbreath.h rather than keeping all prototypes in it_27CF.h.
Item function declarations retain address-comment prefixes such as /* 2C720C */ and /* 3F7938 */ for searchable mapping.
Semantic typedefs/enums like Fighter_Part are preferred over raw s32 when the parameter meaning is known.
Callbacks that operate on item game objects should use Item_GObj* rather than void* when the type is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promoted itgamewatchbreath.c to Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py after source adjustments.
Added explicit stack padding locals: int pad[1] in it_802C7340 and int pad[3] in it_802C744C.
Split rand_int = HSD_Randi(...) + constant into rand_int = HSD_Randi(...); rand_int += constant; in it_8026F8B4.
Changed an unrolled four-hitbox check into a for loop with break in it_802758D4.
Changed damage comparison order to if (damage <= hitcapsule->damage), likely to influence generated compare/branch ordering.
Declared it_803F7938 directly in itgamewatchbreath.c as the ItemStateTable used by the breath item.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: The docs build was likely failing because the it_802C720C header prototype used Item_GObj* while the .c definition used HSD_GObj*. Reviewer suggested using HSD_GObj* for the first argument and Fighter_Part for the third argument.
review_feedback.resolution_evidenced: The final diff removes it_802C720C from it_27CF.h and declares it in itgamewatchbreath.h as HSD_GObj* it_802C720C(HSD_GObj*, Vec3*, Fighter_Part, float).

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching itgamewatchbreath.c to see whether padding locals in it_802C7340 or it_802C744C were removed by better typing or struct recovery.
Check whether other Game & Watch item headers still keep item-specific prototypes in it_27CF.h and should be split similarly.
Review callers of it_802C720C to confirm whether the parent argument is always a fighter HSD_GObj or can be narrowed to Fighter_GObj in future cleanup.
Search for other item callbacks declared as void* that can be safely typed as Item_GObj*.
